question: For safety reasons, --------- must be stored in sealed containers underground.
Majeda will look after your baby well; she's reliable, responsible and completely ---------.

This year, I won't be able to buy a present for my daughter in her birthday. It's ---------.

All people are ---------, deserving the same rights as each other.
Does anyone have any --------- ideas for Maha's surprise family party?
--------- means not in use any more because a newer invention exists.

The --------- of injury or even death is very high for base-jumping wing-suit flyers.

The tickets had scarcely gone on sale when they were sold out. The correct negative inversion of the above sentence is ---------
choices: Scarcely had the tickets gone on sale when they were sold out.
Scarcely gone the tickets had on sale when they were sold out.
Scarcely the tickets on sale had gone when they were sold out.
Scarcely had gone the tickets on sale when they were sold out.

question: I live near --------- River Thames which flows through --------- London.
The police managed to find the child in time. The underlined verb in the above sentence is used to indicate ---------.
'Why are you leaving early?' The correct reported speech form of the question above is ---------
choices: Khalid asked us why we are leaving early.
Khalid asked us why were we leaving early?
Khalid asked us why are we leaving early?
Khalid asked us why we were leaving early.

question: The students apologised --------- not listening to the teacher.
I would really like --------- by one of the giant tech companies.
Some people have suggested that children should not have mobile phones. The sentence that has the correct impersonal passive form of the one above is ---------
choices: It has been suggested by some people that children should not have mobile phones.
It has suggested that children should not have mobile phones.
It had been suggested that children should not have mobile phones.
It had suggested that children should not have mobile phones.

question: If only we --------- where you were, we'd come to get you.
Ali could have given Fadi a lift into town. This sentence means ---------
choices: Fadi did get a lift into town from Ali.
Fadi didn't get a lift into town from Ali.
Fadi doesn't get a lift into town from Ali.
Fadi gets a lift into town from Ali.

question: The letter (i) in the word (extinct) is pronounced the same as in the word ---------.
